pattern_name: Present Simple (He / She / It) — The -s Rule
pattern_name_ar: المضارع البسيط (هو / هي) — قاعدة الـ s
when_to_use: When talking about ONE other person (he, she, it, my mother, Ahmad, the teacher), add -s to the verb.
when_to_use_ar: لمّا تتكلم عن شخص واحد آخر (هو، هي، أمي، أحمد)، أضف s للفعل.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Arabic doesn't add anything to the verb for third person. English requires -s, and forgetting it is the #1 error for Arabic speakers at this level.

the_rule.base: Most verbs: add -s (likes, wants, works, lives)
the_rule.es_rule: Verbs ending in -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o: add -es (goes, watches, washes, does)
the_rule.y_rule: Verbs ending in consonant + y: change y to -ies (studies, carries, tries)

common_errors.explanation: ALWAYS add -s for he/she/it. This is the most common mistake.
have is irregular: have → has (not haves).

negative_form.critical_note: After 'doesn't', the verb goes BACK to base form. No -s! The 's' moves to 'does'.
negative_form.critical_note_ar: بعد doesn't، الفعل يرجع لشكله الأساسي. بدون s!

question_form.critical_note: Same rule: after 'does', verb = base form. No -s.

quick_rule: He/She/It = add -s to the verb. ALWAYS. Exception: after doesn't/does = base form.
quick_rule_ar: هو/هي = أضف s للفعل. دائماً. الاستثناء: بعد doesn't أو does = الشكل الأساسي.
mnemonic: He SpeakS, She WorkS, It RainS — the S follows he/she/it everywhere!
connect_to_speaking: When describing your family: 'My mother WORKS. My father DRIVES. My sister STUDIES.' Practice the -s sound!
